hooks.command: Write-Host '=== security-reviewer hook system initialized ===' -ForegroundColor Red
Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Deep security audit agent active. Model: claude-opus-4-6. Covers: OWASP Top 10, CWE Top 25, language-specific patterns.' -ForegroundColor Red
Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Supported languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Ruby, Java, C#, Go, PHP, Kotlin, Swift, Rust, Solidity, Move.' -ForegroundColor Cyan

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Analysis focus: (1) Trust boundary & data flow, (2) AuthN/AuthZ, (3) Injection vectors, (4) Cryptography misuse, (5) Deserialization, (6) SSRF paths, (7) Business logic flaws.' -ForegroundColor Magenta

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Audit modes: (1) Full repo scan (slow, comprehensive), (2) Targeted file review (fast, focused), (3) PR/diff review (incremental), (4) Architecture diagram review (design-time).' -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Output schema: security_audit/{audit_id}.json — contains: findings[], trust_boundaries[], data_flow_diagram, summary_metrics, recommendations[].' -ForegroundColor Gray

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Finding severity: CRITICAL (RCE, SQLi, auth bypass), HIGH (SSRF, IDOR, XSS stored), MEDIUM (XSS reflected, CSRF, info leak), LOW (security misconfig, missing headers), INFO (best practice).' -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Language-specific checklists loaded: see rules/security-reviewer-{lang}.md for each language pattern guide.' -ForegroundColor Gray
Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Scope: black-box (runtime behavior) + white-box (source code) combined for maximum coverage.' -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Trust boundary rule: every public-facing input is a potential attack surface. Trace data flow from input to sink.' -ForegroundColor Yellow

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Analysis scope options: full repository (deepest, slowest), targeted path (focused, fast), PR diff only (incremental), external attack surface (black-box). Default: targeted path unless full scan requested.' -ForegroundColor Cyan

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Trust boundary mapping: identify data trust levels (public, internal, confidential, restricted). Map data flow across layers (client -> API -> service -> DB -> cloud). Each boundary crossing = potential breach point.' -ForegroundColor Magenta

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Attack surface enumeration: list all input vectors (HTTP params, headers, body, file uploads, cookies, WebSocket messages, GraphQL queries, file reads, env vars, config files, framework internals).' -ForegroundColor Cyan

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Code path analysis: for each input vector, trace data flow forward (taint analysis) and backward (source analysis). Identify: sanitization points, validation gates, serialization/deserialization, encoding/decoding.' -ForegroundColor Gray

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Business logic section: review auth flows (register, login, password reset, MFA, session management), financial operations (payments, refunds, transfers, credits), access control (role changes, permissions, admin operations), data operations (CRUD with user isolation).' -ForegroundColor Yellow

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] CWE Top 25 extended: CWE-787 (OOB Write), CWE-79 (XSS), CWE-89 (SQLi), CWE-20 (Improper Input Validation), CWE-78 (OS Command Injection), CWE-416 (Use After Free), CWE-22 (Path Traversal), CWE-352 (CSRF), CWE-434 (Unrestricted File Upload), CWE-502 (Deserialization).' -ForegroundColor Yellow

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Priority levels: CRITICAL = RCE, SQLi, auth bypass, hardcoded prod creds. HIGH = SSRF, stored XSS, IDOR, insecure deserialization. MEDIUM = reflected XSS, CSRF, info leak, weak crypto. LOW = missing security headers, verbose errors. INFO = best practice.' -ForegroundColor Yellow

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Trust boundary classification: PUBLIC (no auth required), INTERNAL (auth required but no privilege), AUTHENTICATED (logged-in user), PRIVILEGED (admin), RESTRICTED (system/service). Each boundary crossing is auditable.' -ForegroundColor Yellow

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER] Recurring vuln pattern library maintained: SQLi patterns (ORM bypass, HQL injection, NoSQL injection), XSS patterns (DOM, reflected, stored, prototype pollution), SSRF patterns (redirect, DNS rebinding, cloud metadata).' -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Pre-flight security check: verifying target file paths, tool identity, and auth context before any code or infra access.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

if ($lastCommand -match 'cat\.json|cat\.yaml|cat\.yml|Get-Content\.json|Get-Content\.yaml|type\.json|cat.*config') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Config file read. Check for: hardcoded secrets, default credentials, weak TLS config, overly permissive CORS, debug mode flags, exposed admin endpoints.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'semgrep|semgrep.*scan|semgrep.*cli|semgrep.*--config') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Semgrep SAST detected. Using rule sets: p/security-audit, p/secrets, p/owasp-top-ten, p/cwe-top-25. Supplement with manual review for business logic.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'bandit|bandit.*-r|bandit.*scan|bandit.*report') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Bandit (Python SAST) detected. Scanning for: eval/exec, pickle, subprocess, SQL injection, path traversal, hardcoded passwords, weak crypto.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'brakeman|brakeman.*scan|brakeman.*-q') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Brakeman (Ruby SAST) detected. Scanning for: SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, mass assignment, redirect, command injection, unsafe deserialization.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'gosec|gosec.*scan|gosec.*./...') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Gosec (Go SAST) detected. Scanning for: command injection, path traversal, weak crypto, hardcoded credentials, SSRF, unsafe parsing, file permissions.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'spotbugs|findsecbugs|spotbugs.*-effort') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] SpotBugs + FindSecBugs (Java) detected. Scanning for: SQLi, XSS, path traversal, SSRF, insecure random, weak crypto, deserialization, JNDI injection.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'npm audit|yarn audit|pnpm audit|audit.*fix|audit.*--json') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] NPM audit detected. Scanning package.json dependencies for known CVEs via npm registry advisory database.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'pip audit|pip-audit|requirements.*txt|pyproject\.toml|Pipfile') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] pip-audit detected. Scanning Python dependencies via PyPI advisory database and OSV.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'govulncheck|osv-scanner|grype|syft|trivy|anchore|snyk test') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Go/container vulnerability scanner detected. Scanning: binary dependencies, base images, language ecosystems (Go, Python, Node, Java, Ruby).' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'checkov|checkov.*-d|checkov.*--framework|tfsec|trivy.*config|datree') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] IaC scanner detected (Checkov/tfsec/Trivy). Checking: public S3, open security groups, unencrypted storage, IAM wildcards, privileged containers, hardcoded secrets in Terraform/K8s.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'detect-secrets|truffleHog|gitleaks|git-secrets|trufflehog.*scan') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Secret scanning tool detected. Scanning: git history, codebase, config files. Plugins: AWS, GCP, Azure, Slack, GitHub, Stripe, Twilio. Output: findings/security-reviewer/secrets/' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'CodeQL|codeql|codeql.*database|codeql.*analyze|codeql.*query') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] GitHub CodeQL detected. Running: cpp, csharp, go, java, javascript, python, ruby, swift queries. Deep semantic analysis, not just pattern matching.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'eslint.*security|eslint-plugin-security|eslint.*--plugin') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] ESLint security plugin detected. Rules: detect-eval-with-expression, detect-possible-timing-attacks, no-new-array, no-jquery/redirect, no-pseudo-random, detect-object-injection.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'sqlmap|nosqlmap|commix|tplmap|patator|ffuf|wfuzz') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Active injection scanner detected. Verify explicit authorization. Ensure target in scope. Save all scan output to findings/security-reviewer/active_scans/.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'docker scan|docker scout|trivy.*image|snyk container|anchore') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Container image scan detected. Checking: base image CVEs, exposed ports, running processes, filesystem permissions, secrets in layers, capabilities, user privileges.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }
Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Verifying target files/directories are within project scope before reading.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

if ($lastCommand -match 'cat|type|Get-Content|Read|head|tail|less|more') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] File read detected. Scanning for security anti-patterns in: auth, crypto, SQL, XSS, SSRF, deserialization, access control.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'grep|rg|Select-String|findstr|egrep|ack') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Pattern search in files. Checking for dangerous functions and insecure patterns across codebase.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'semgrep|bandit|brakeman|gosec|spotbugs|findsecbugs|eslint.*security|tflint|tfsec|checkov') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] SAST tool detected. Running automated pattern checks. Supplement with manual review for business logic bugs.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'npm audit|pip audit|bundle audit|govulncheck|osv-scanner|snyk|dependabot') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Dependency vulnerability scan detected. Results feed into overall risk assessment.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'docker|Dockerfile|docker-compose|k8s|kubernetes|helm|terraform|pulumi') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Infrastructure-as-code/config detected. Check for: privileged containers, public S3, open security groups, hardcoded secrets.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }

if ($lastCommand -match 'curl|httpie|burp|zaproxy|postman|insomnia|hoppscotch') { Write-Host '[SEC-REV PRE] Runtime/black-box test detected. Ensure test requests are logged for evidence trail.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed }
Write-Host '[SEC-REV POST] Reviewing security analysis results and compiling finding entries.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] SAST tool orchestration: run all tools in parallel: semgrep (linting), bandit/gosec (lang-specific), osv-scanner (deps), gitleaks (secrets), CodeQL (deep semantic). Combine results, deduplicate, prioritize. Manual review fills gaps.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

if ($lastOutput -match 'class.*ForName|ClassLoader|defineClass|loadClass|URLClassLoader|MLet|MBeanServer|JMX|remote method') { Write-Host '[JNDI/REFLECTION] Java class loading/reflection detected. If attacker controls class name or classpath, JNDI lookup or remote class loading = RCE vector via unsafe ObjectInputStream or javax.naming.Context lookup.' -ForegroundColor Red }

if ($lastOutput -match 'ProcessBuilder|Process\.start|Runtime\.getRuntime| exec |syscall|system\(|passthru|shell_exec|popen|proc_open|pcntl_exec') { Write-Host '[OS CMD] OS command execution vector. CRITICAL if user input reaches here without sanitization. Check: (1) exec with string concat = dangerous, (2) exec with array = safer but still risky, (3) shell metacharacter filtering, (4) allowlist-based command selection.' -ForegroundColor Red }

if ($lastOutput -match 'XMLDecoder|XStream|XStream.*fromXML|XMLInputFactory|SAXParserFactory|DocumentBuilderFactory|TransformerFactory|SchemaFactory') { Write-Host '[XXE/XML] XML parsing framework detected. Java specifically: XMLDecoder = deserialization RCE if attacker-controlled XML. XStream = XXE + deserialization. DocumentBuilderFactory = XXE. Disable external entities (setFeature http://apache.org/xml/features/disallow-doctype-decl).' -ForegroundColor Red }

if ($lastOutput -match 'GraphQL|graphql|__schema|__type|introspection|mutation|subscription|query.*\{') { Write-Host '[GRAPHQL] GraphQL endpoint detected. Check: (1) introspection enabled in production, (2) query depth/complexity limits, (3) query cost analysis, (4) batching attack (1000 queries in one request), (5) authorization per resolver (not just per field).' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'password_hash|password_verify|bcrypt|argon2|scrypt|PBKDF2|SHA-256.*salt|salt.*SHA-256') { Write-Host '[PWD HASH] Password hashing found. Verify: (1) using bcrypt/argon2/scrypt (not MD5/SHA1), (2) sufficient cost factor (bcrypt cost >= 12), (3) unique salt per password, (4) pepper in application layer. Weak password hashing = credential compromise risk.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'set-cookie|Set-Cookie|session.*cookie|token.*cookie|remember.*cookie|Secure.*Cookie|HttpOnly|SameSite') { Write-Host '[COOKIE] Cookie security analysis. Check: (1) Secure flag set (HTTPS only), (2) HttpOnly flag (JS access blocked), (3) SameSite=Strict/Lax, (4) __Host- prefix for sensitive cookies, (5) cookie expiration reasonable, (6) no session fixation risk.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'X-Forwarded-For|X-Real-IP|Forwarded|X-Originating-IP|X-Remote-Addr|X-Client-IP|X-Cluster-Client-IP') { Write-Host '[IP SPOOF] IP header handling found. If app trusts X-Forwarded-For without validation, IP spoofing possible. Check: (1) which proxy populates these headers, (2) internal-only proxy, (3) header removed at edge, (4) rate limiting uses real IP.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'include\(|require\(|load|import|autoload|composer|django.*urls|url.*patterns|route.*file') { Write-Host '[LFI/RCE] File inclusion vector found. PHP include/require with user input = LFI/RCE. Python import with user input = RCE. Check: (1) input sanitized/allowlisted, (2) chroot/jail enforced, (3) no directory traversal, (4) extension validation.' -ForegroundColor Red }

if ($lastOutput -match 'mysql_|mysqli_|PDO|pg_|sqlite|mongo|redis|Memcache|memcached|elastic|solr|kafka|rabbit') { Write-Host '[DB] Database interaction found. Check: (1) parameterized queries, (2) least privilege DB user, (3) query timeout enforced, (4) result set size limited, (5) error messages not leaking schema, (6) connection encryption (TLS), (7) credentials in config not in code.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'S3|s3|boto3|aws.*sdk|google.*storage|azure.*blob|object.*storage|rclone|s3cmd|aws.*cli') { Write-Host '[CLOUD STORAGE] Cloud storage SDK/API found. Check: (1) bucket ACLs (public/private), (2) bucket policy allows ListBucket, (3) object permissions, (4) server-side encryption, (5) versioning enabled, (6) access logging, (7) pre-signed URL expiry. Public bucket = immediate report.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'kafka|kinesis|eventhub|pubsub|rabbitmq|activemq|sqs|sqs.*aws|redis.*pub|channel|topic') { Write-Host '[MSG QUEUE] Message queue detected. Check: (1) authentication per channel, (2) message encryption, (3) consumer group authorization, (4) message TTL enforcement, (5) dead-letter queue secured, (6) message replay prevention, (7) poison message handling.' -ForegroundColor Gray }

if ($lastOutput -match 'YAML\.load|yaml\.load|load\(|@@yaml|SnakeYAML|Yaml\.load|ruamel|yaml\.unsafe_load|unsafe_load') { Write-Host '[DESER YAML] YAML deserialization found. PyYAML yaml.load() = unsafe by default. SnakeYAML in Java = unsafe constructor. Exploit: create YAML with !!python/object tag -> arbitrary object instantiation. Fix: use yaml.safe_load() / SafeConstructor.' -ForegroundColor Red }

if ($lastOutput -match 'JNDI|InitialContext|lookup|ldap://|ldaps://|rmi://|corba|dns:|Naming\.getInitialContext|DirContext') { Write-Host '[JNDI LOOKUP] JNDI lookup vector found. Log4Shell-class vulnerability. If attacker controls URL passed to JNDI lookup = RCE. Check: (1) is lookup call with user input, (2) can protocol be ldap/rmi, (3) are JNDI lookups disabled in JVM (-Dcom.sun.jndi.ldap.object.trustURLCodebase=false).' -ForegroundColor Red }

if ($lastOutput -match 'csrf|CSRF|csrf_token|X-CSRF-Token|xsrf|XSRF-TOKEN|_csrf|VerifyCsrfToken|CsrfToken') { Write-Host '[CSRF] CSRF protection analysis. Check: (1) all state-changing routes protected, (2) token unpredictable per session, (3) SameSite cookie attribute set, (4) token not in URL (referrer leak), (5) custom headers enforced (non-simple CORS preflight), (6) token rotates per request for sensitive ops.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'X-Frame-Options|frame-ancestors|Content-Security-Policy.*frame|clickjack|UI redress|drag.*drop') { Write-Host '[CLICKJACK] Clickjacking protection analysis. Check: (1) X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN present, (2) CSP frame-ancestors present, (3) both present = defense in depth, (4) sensitive endpoints (settings/payment/admin) protected, (5) frameguard middleware active.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'X-Content-Type-Options|nosniff|MIME.*type|sniff|content.type|mime.*magic') { Write-Host '[MIME SNIFF] MIME sniffing protection analysis. Check: (1) X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on all responses, (2) Content-Type accurately set, (3) SVG images have Content-Type: image/svg+xml (not text/html), (4) JSON responses have application/json, not text/html. Missing nosniff = IE/XSS vector.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'cache|Cache-Control|no-store|no-cache|private|public|max-age|s-maxage|etag|last-modified|vary') { Write-Host '[CACHE] Cache control analysis. Sensitive responses MUST have: Cache-Control: no-store, no-cache, private. Public = browser caches sensitive data. Missing vary = cached cross-user responses (info leak). Check if authenticated responses are cached by CDN.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'json|JsonResponse|JSON|jsonify|response.*json|JSON.stringify|JSON.parse|JSONB|JSONColumn') { Write-Host '[JSON] JSON serialization found. Check: (1) JSON injection possible if user input in JSON string without escaping, (2) JSON hijacking via CSRF + content-type application/json + array response, (3) sensitive data in JSON responses, (4) JSONP callbacks with user input = XSS.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'sleep|usleep|time_sleep_until|Thread\.sleep|delay|setTimeout|pause|wait') { Write-Host '[TIMING] Timing function found. Potential for timing attacks if used in security-sensitive comparisons. Password comparison, token validation, auth checks must use constant-time comparison. Variable-time operations leak information about secret values.' -ForegroundColor Gray }

if ($lastOutput -match 'assert|assertEquals|assertThat|expect.*toBe|should|chai\.expect|Jest\.expect') { Write-Host '[TEST SECRET] Test/assertion code found in production. Test code may contain: (1) hardcoded test credentials, (2) debug endpoints left open, (3) verbose error messages, (4) mock/stub implementations that bypass checks. Test code should not ship to production.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'TODO|FIXME|HACK|XXX|WARN|DEPRECATED|WORKAROUND') { Write-Host '[CODE QUALITY] Technical debt markers found. Security-relevant: TODOs near auth checks, FIXME in crypto code, HACK in authz logic. These indicate incomplete security implementations. Each flagged for manual review by security-reviewer.' -ForegroundColor Gray }

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Defensive coding analysis: check for (1) centralized error handling (no stack traces to users), (2) centralized auth/authz (no per-route reimplementation), (3) centralized input validation (no per-field reimplementation), (4) security tests in CI/CD, (5) code review for security changes.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Architecture decision records (ADR) review: if ADRs exist, check security implications of documented architectural decisions. ADRs should document: threat model, trust boundaries, security controls, compliance requirements. Missing security ADRs = architectural risk gap.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] Audit complete. Verify: all code paths traced? trust boundaries documented? findings prioritized? remediation guidance provided? Supply chain verified? Compliance mapped?' -ForegroundColor Red

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] Next: /validator for each finding, then /report-writer with audit package (code snippets, PoC, impact). Schedule re-audit per SLA for each finding severity.' -ForegroundColor Green

command: if ($lastOutput -match 'class.*ForName|ClassLoader|defineClass|loadClass|URLClassLoader|MLet|MBeanServer|JMX|remote method') { Write-Host '[JNDI/REFLECTION] Java class loading/reflection detected. If app trusts X-Forwarded-For without validation, IP spoofing possible. Check: (1) parameterized queries, (2) least privilege DB user, (3) query timeout, (4) result set limited, (5) error messages not leaking schema, (6) connection encryption (TLS), (7) credentials not in code.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'S3|s3|boto3|aws.*sdk|storage.googleapis|azure.*blob|rclone|s3cmd') { Write-Host '[CLOUD STORAGE] Cloud storage SDK/API found. Check: (1) bucket ACLs, (2) ListBucket policy, (3) object permissions, (4) server-side encryption, (5) versioning, (6) access logging, (7) pre-signed URL expiry. Public bucket = immediate report.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'kafka|kinesis|sqs|rabbitmq|activemq|redis.*pub|channel') { Write-Host '[MSG QUEUE] Message queue detected. Check: (1) auth per channel, (2) message encryption, (3) consumer authz, (4) message TTL, (5) DLQ secured, (6) replay prevention, (7) poison message handling.' -ForegroundColor Gray }

if ($lastOutput -match 'YAML\.load|yaml\.load|SnakeYAML|yaml\.unsafe_load|unsafe_load') { Write-Host '[DESER YAML] YAML deserialization found. PyYAML yaml.load() = unsafe. SnakeYAML = unsafe constructor. Exploit: !!python/object tag -> arbitrary instantiation. Fix: yaml.safe_load() / SafeConstructor.' -ForegroundColor Red }

if ($lastOutput -match 'JNDI|InitialContext|lookup|ldap://|rmi://|dns:|Naming\.getInitialContext|DirContext') { Write-Host '[JNDI LOOKUP] JNDI lookup vector found. Log4Shell-class. If attacker controls URL = RCE. Check: (1) user input in lookup, (2) can protocol be ldap/rmi, (3) JNDI lookups disabled in JVM (-Dcom.sun.jndi.ldap.object.trustURLCodebase=false).' -ForegroundColor Red }

if ($lastOutput -match 'csrf|CSRF|csrf_token|X-CSRF-Token|xsrf|XSRF-TOKEN|VerifyCsrfToken') { Write-Host '[CSRF] CSRF protection analysis. Check: (1) all state-changing routes protected, (2) token unpredictable, (3) SameSite cookie, (4) token not in URL, (5) custom headers enforced, (6) token rotates per request for sensitive ops.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'X-Frame-Options|frame-ancestors|Content-Security-Policy.*frame|clickjack') { Write-Host '[CLICKJACK] Clickjacking protection. Check: (1) X-Frame-Options present, (2) CSP frame-ancestors present, (3) sensitive endpoints protected, (4) frameguard middleware active.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'X-Content-Type-Options|nosniff|MIME.*type|sniff|content.type') { Write-Host '[MIME SNIFF] MIME sniffing protection. Check: (1) nosniff on all responses, (2) Content-Type accurate, (3) SVG = image/svg+xml, (4) JSON = application/json. Missing nosniff = IE/XSS vector.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'Cache-Control|no-store|no-cache|private|public|max-age|etag|vary') { Write-Host '[CACHE] Cache control analysis. Sensitive responses MUST have Cache-Control: no-store. Public = browser caches sensitive data. Missing vary = cross-user response caching (info leak).' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'json|JsonResponse|JSON|jsonify|response.*json|JSON.stringify|JSON.parse|JSONB') { Write-Host '[JSON] JSON serialization found. Check: (1) JSON injection with unescaped user input, (2) JSON hijacking via CSRF + content-type application/json + array response, (3) sensitive data in JSON, (4) JSONP callbacks with user input = XSS.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'sleep|usleep|Thread\.sleep|delay|setTimeout') { Write-Host '[TIMING] Timing function found. Timing attacks leak secret info. Password comparison, token validation = must use constant-time comparison.' -ForegroundColor Gray }

if ($lastOutput -match 'assert|assertEquals|Jest\.expect|chai\.expect|should') { Write-Host '[TEST CODE] Test code in production. May contain hardcoded test credentials, debug endpoints, verbose errors, mock implementations. Test code should not ship to production.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'TODO|FIXME|HACK|XXX|WARN|DEPRECATED') { Write-Host '[CODE QUALITY] Technical debt markers. Security-relevant: TODOs near auth checks, FIXME in crypto, HACK in authz logic. Flagged for manual security-reviewer review.' -ForegroundColor Gray }

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Defensive coding analysis: check for (1) centralized error handling, (2) centralized auth/authz, (3) centralized input validation, (4) security tests in CI/CD, (5) code review for security changes.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Architecture decision records (ADR) review: if ADRs exist, check security implications. ADRs should document: threat model, trust boundaries, security controls. Missing security ADRs = architectural risk gap.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Compliance mapping: findings mapped to PCI-DSS (6.5 code review, 6.6 WAF), SOC2 (CC7.1-CC7.4), HIPAA (164.312), GDPR (Article 32), ISO27001 (A.14.2). Include compliance context in security-audit reports.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Remediation guidance: every finding must include (1) Root cause, (2) Affected versions/files, (3) Recommended fix with code example, (4) OWASP reference link, (5) Verification steps, (6) Estimated effort (story points).' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Finding deduplication: before adding finding, check if (1) same CWE exists, (2) same affected component, (3) same attack vector. If match -> merge evidence. False duplicates = separate by code path.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] SAST tool orchestration: run all tools in parallel: semgrep (linting), bandit/gosec (lang-specific), osv-scanner (deps), gitleaks (secrets), CodeQL (deep semantic). Combine, deduplicate, prioritize. Manual review fills gaps.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Attack surface summary format: each finding includes {title, severity, confidence, category, affected_component, attack_vector, prerequisites, user_interaction, cwe_id, owasp_category, remediation, cvss_40_estimate}.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Trust boundary diagram format: Mermaid ERD showing: internet -> WAF/CDN -> load balancer -> API gateway -> service mesh -> microservices -> databases. Label each boundary crossing with auth mechanism and encryption status.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Integration with p1-warrior: security-reviewer finds bugs in source = p1-warrior validates runtime. Process: Reviewer identifies pattern -> Warrior tests via HTTP -> Validator confirms -> Report written.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Integration with js-analysis: JS analysis finds client-side issues. Security-reviewer correlates these with server-side code, finds root cause rather than symptom.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Integration with autopilot: security-reviewer runs as Phase-1b (after recon+ranker). Findings feed directly into p1-warrior target list. Critical findings auto-promote to P1.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] False positive handling: mark as false_positive with reason. Common reasons: theoretical, insane prerequisites, admin-only, unsupported browser. False positive needs tech lead sign-off.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Integration with exploit-researcher: for vulnerabilities with CVE IDs, pass CVE list to exploit-researcher for PoC availability check. Enrich security audit findings with PoC context.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Output format: SARIF v2.1 for tool integration, JSON for internal consumption, Markdown for human review. SARIF enables GitHub Security tab, GitLab SAST reports, IDE inline annotations.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Audit trail logging: security_audit/{audit_id}/audit_log.jsonl tracks every tool invocation, finding created/updated/closed. Each entry: timestamp, action, tool, result_hash, affected_findings.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Technical debt SLA: Critical = 14 days, High = 30 days, Medium = 90 days, Low = 180 days. SLA overdue = escalation to engineering leadership. Persistent unfixed critical at 90 days = CISO escalation.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Secure SDLC integration: threat modeling (STRIDE/PASTA) at design phase, SAST in CI, DAST in staging, dependency scan per PR, pen test pre-release, response plan maintained. Each phase has security exit criteria.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Cloud-native security: shared responsibility model, IAM least privilege, VPC/security groups, KMS for encryption, secrets manager, container scanning, image signing, service mesh mTLS, runtime security (Falco).' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Supply chain analysis: scan dependency manifests (package.json, requirements.txt, pom.xml, Gemfile, go.mod, Cargo.toml). Use OSV-Scanner, Snyk, Grype. Output: sbom.json + vulnerable_components.json.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] API security checklist: auth (OAuth2 with PKCE), authz (RBAC/ABAC), input validation (JSON schema), rate limiting, pagination limits, field-level encryption, audit logging, versioning, deprecated endpoint handling, webhook signature verification.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Business logic review focus: auth flows (register, login, password reset, MFA, session), financial ops (payments, refunds, transfers), access control (role changes, admin ops), data operations (CRUD with user isolation). Automated scanners miss these.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Regression analysis: compare current findings to previous audit. New = regression, fixed = improvement, persistent = unfilled SLA. Delta report: new, fixed, unchanged, aged findings per program.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Documentation requirements: (1) executive summary, (2) technical findings, (3) SAST/DAST raw output, (4) trust boundary diagram, (5) remediation roadmap with effort estimates. All stored in /security-audit/{audit_id}/.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Report distribution: executive summary = leadership/board. Technical report = engineering team. Raw SARIF = security tools. Findings summary = bug bounty report (sanitized).' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Framework-specific audits: Node.js/Express -> check helmet, rate limiting, JWT. Python/Django -> CSRF, debug mode, secret key. Java/Spring -> @PreAuthorize, SQL via JdbcTemplate, JNDI. .NET -> Process.Start, BinaryFormatter, path traversal.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Container/cloud audits: Kubernetes -> pod security standards, RBAC, privileged containers. AWS -> IAM roles, S3 ACLs, security groups, KMS. Database -> SQL mode, least privilege, TLS, encryption at rest.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Auth audit: password hashing (bcrypt/argon2, not MD5), session token entropy, JWT algorithm, token expiration, refresh rotation, MFA, brute force protection, account lockout, password policy.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SEC-REV CTX] Data protection audit: encryption at rest (AES-256), encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+), PII classification, data retention, right-to-erasure, GDPR/CCPA, backup encryption, key rotation schedule.' -ForegroundColor DarkRed

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] Audit finalization: (1) trust boundaries documented, (2) all findings verified with code references, (3) SARIF report generated, (4) remediation validated, (5) executive summary drafted, (6) raw files archived.' -ForegroundColor Red

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] Schedule re-audit: critical=90 days, high=180 days, medium=365 days. Track fix rate in audit history. Unfixed critical at 90 days = CISO escalation.' -ForegroundColor Red

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] Final integrity check: SHA-256 hashes for audit.json and findings.json. Sign and date all deliverables. Report delivered to /security-audit/{audit_id}/.' -ForegroundColor Red

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] SecuritySUMMARY.md written: 10-page summary for CISO. Includes: risk score, critical findings count, SLA compliance, recommended actions with timeline.' -ForegroundColor Green

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] Follow-up actions: (1) open Jira tickets for each unfixed finding, (2) assign owners, (3) set due dates per SLA, (4) schedule weekly triage meeting, (5) report to CISO monthly on fix progress.' -ForegroundColor Green

Write-Host '[SEC-REV] DevSecOps culture assessment: check if team practices shift-left security, security champions program, bug bounty program engagement, security training completion rate, secure coding standards documented and enforced.' -ForegroundColor Yellow

Write-Host '[SEC-REV] Incident response readiness: check if security incidents have: (1) documented runbook, (2) defined roles, (3) communication plan, (4) forensic capabilities, (5) backup recovery tested, (6) lessons learned process. Gaps = security posture risk.' -ForegroundColor Red

Write-Host '[SEC-REV] Third-party risk: vendor code, SDKs, APIs have their own security posture. Check: (1) vendor security audit available, (2) software composition analysis, (3) vendor SLA for security incidents, (4) right to audit clause, (5) data processing agreement.' -ForegroundColor Yellow

if ($lastOutput -match 'saml|SAML|SAMLResponse|SAMLRequest|IdP|SP|Assertion|AttributeConsumingService') { Write-Host '[SAML] SAML SSO implementation detected. Check: (1) XML signature validation strict, (2) assertion not replayed, (3) audience restriction validated, (4) encryption of assertions, (5) NameID format appropriate, (6) clock skew tolerance reasonable (< 5 min).' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'jwt|JWT|jsonwebtoken|TokenAuthentication|OAuth2|Bearer|Authorization.*Bearer') { Write-Host '[JWT] JWT implementation detected. Check: (1) algorithm validated (not alg:none), (2) signature required, (3) expiration (exp) enforced, (4) not-before (nbf) enforced, (5) audience/issuer validated, (6) strong secret/key, (7) token rotation.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'stripe|braintree|square|paypal|adyen|razorpay|payment|checkout|billing') { Write-Host '[PAYMENT] Payment processing detected. High-value target. Check: (1) payment amounts server-validated, (2) status transitions server-controlled, (3) webhook signature verified, (4) currency enforced, (5) discount/coupon server-side, (6) idempotency keys for retries.' -ForegroundColor Red }

if ($lastOutput -match 'session|Session|createSession|getSession|setAttribute|getAttribute|invalidate|destroy') { Write-Host '[SESSION] Session management detected. Check: (1) session ID entropy, (2) session ID rotation on auth state change, (3) session expiration (idle + absolute), (4) concurrent session limits, (5) session fixation prevention, (6) session store secured.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'two_factor|2FA|2fa|totp|hotp|webauthn|FIDO2|backup.*code|recovery.*code') { Write-Host '[MFA] Multi-factor authentication detected. Check: (1) MFA required for sensitive operations, (2) MFA bypass via API endpoints without MFA check, (3) backup codes entropy, (4) recovery flow security, (5) MFA session persistence reasonable.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'email|verify|confirm|reset.*token|magic.*link|passwordless|otp') { Write-Host '[AUTH FLOW] Email-based auth flow detected. Check: (1) token entropy, (2) token single-use, (3) token expiration, (4) rate limiting, (5) brute force protection, (6) token in URL (referrer leak risk), (7) account enumeration via response differences.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'callback|webhook|notify|event|hook|subscription|freshdesk|zendesk|intercom|drift') { Write-Host '[WEBHOOK] Webhook/callback system detected. Check: (1) signature verification on incoming, (2) signature on outgoing, (3) replay protection, (4) timeout/retry logic, (5) error handling doesn't leak info, (6) rate limiting on webhook endpoints.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'admin|Admin|ADMIN|dashboard|manage|console|panel|control|setup|config') { Write-Host '[ADMIN] Admin/management interface detected. Check: (1) IP allowlist for admin, (2) MFA required, (3) audit logging of all admin actions, (4) no default credentials, (5) admin endpoints not discoverable, (6) admin session timeout shorter than regular.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'export|download|backup|snapshot|dump|archive|import|bulk|mass.*action') { Write-Host '[DATA OPS] Bulk data operation detected. Check: (1) authorization per record, (2) rate limiting, (3) export format safe (no formula injection in CSV), (4) audit logging, (5) PII not in export filenames, (6) export watermark/tracking for leaked data forensics.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'event|listener|observer|pub.*sub|emit|on\(|addEventListener|subscribe|publish') { Write-Host '[EVENT] Event system detected. Check: (1) event naming doesn't leak sensitive info, (2) event payloads not exposing internals, (3) event handler authz not just event producer, (4) event replay attack possible, (5) event serialization secure.' -ForegroundColor Gray }

if ($lastOutput -match 'i18n|localization|locale|language|translate|lang=|Accept-Language') { Write-Host '[I18N] Internationalization detected. Check: (1) locale not used for security decisions, (2) translated strings dont introduce XSS, (3) locale-based content doesn't leak PII, (4) right-to-left languages handled correctly in security controls.' -ForegroundColor Gray }

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] Report delivered. Next steps: (1) engineering team reviews findings, (2) security champions assigned, (3) remediation tickets created in Jira, (4) re-audit scheduled per SLA, (5) executive briefing scheduled.' -ForegroundColor Red

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] Audit archived to: security-audit/{audit_id}/. Checksums verified. Knowledge base updated with new patterns. Hunter community notified of new findings if public program.' -ForegroundColor Green

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] Security posture delta calculated. Overall rating: [A/B/C/D/F]. Delta from last audit: +X/-Y. Top 3 risk areas identified. Board presentation deck generated at security-audit/{audit_id}/board_summary.pptx.' -ForegroundColor Yellow

Write-Host '[SEC-REV] Audit readiness check: before starting audit, confirm: (1) scope document signed off, (2) access credentials to code repos provided, (3) infrastructure access granted, (4) points of contact identified, (5) SLA expectations agreed, (6) communication channels established.' -ForegroundColor Cyan

Write-Host '[SEC-REV] Code quality correlation: security findings correlate with code quality metrics. High cyclomatic complexity = more bugs. Low test coverage = higher risk. High code churn = recent changes need focused review.' -ForegroundColor Gray

Write-Host '[SEC-REV] Audit scope enforcement: only analyze in-scope code and infrastructure. Out-of-scope components may be noted but not deeply analyzed unless relevant to in-scope attack surface.' -ForegroundColor Yellow

Write-Host '[SEC-REV] Finding evidence standards: every finding must have: (1) code snippet with line numbers, (2) taint trace from source to sink, (3) proof of concept (if feasible without production impact), (4) OWASP/CWE reference, (5) remediation code example.' -ForegroundColor Yellow

if ($lastOutput -match 'reflect|Reflect|Reflection|invoke|call|apply|bind') { Write-Host '[REFLECTION] Reflection/reflective calls detected. Reflection bypasses compile-time type checks, can access hidden methods/fields. Check: (1) user input controls class/method name, (2) reflective access to sensitive operations, (3) performance implications, (4) security manager restrictions.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'Promise|Deferred|Future|CompletableFuture|async.*await|then\(|catch\(|finally\(') { Write-Host '[ASYNC] Async control flow detected. Async code has unique bugs: (1) unhandled promise rejections = crashes, (2) race conditions in concurrent async ops, (3) timeout handling inconsistent, (4) error context lost in promise chains, (5) async authz checks bypassed.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'monkeypatch|mock|stub|fake|test.*double|test.*fixture|fixture') { Write-Host '[TEST INFRA] Test infrastructure code found in production path. Monkey patches in production = undefined behavior. Mock implementations bypassing real logic. Test fixtures left in production code = potential backdoors.' -ForegroundColor Red }

if ($lastOutput -match 'global|GLOBAL|window|process\.env|__dirname|__filename|require\.cache|module\.exports') { Write-Host '[STATE] Global/module-level state mutation detected. Global state = attack surface for prototype pollution, prototype chain manipulation. Module exports with sensitive functions = API surface abuse potential.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'os\.(environ|getenv|system)|process\.env|getenv\(|environ\.get|config\[|settings\[') { Write-Host '[ENV] Environment variable access detected. Check: (1) secrets in env vars (should be in secret manager), (2) env vars not validated before use, (3) debug flags via env vars remain active in production, (4) env var injection via process manipulation.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'multipart|FormData|file_uploads|upload_handler|move_uploaded_file|copy\(') { Write-Host '[UPLOAD] File/multipart upload handling detected. Check: (1) file size limits enforced server-side, (2) extension allowlist (not blocklist), (3) MIME validation server-side, (4) filename sanitized, (5) stored outside web root, (6) no path traversal in filename, (7) content scanned for malware.' -ForegroundColor Yellow }

if ($lastOutput -match 'parse\(|deserialize|decode|loads\(|fromJSON|fromXML|fromYAML|valueOf|valueOfString') { Write-Host '[DESER] Deserialization with external data detected. Any deserialization of untrusted data = critical risk. Check: (1) is input trusted, (2) type constraints enforced, (3) allowlist-based deserialization, (4) no polymorphic deserialization, (5) safe alternative available (JSON schema validation).' -ForegroundColor Red }

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] Audit complete. All integration points verified. All output artifacts signed and checksummed. Audit report delivered. Knowledge base updated. Next audit scheduled.' -ForegroundColor Red

Write-Host '[SECURITY-REVIEWER STOP] Final metrics: total findings={count}, critical={count}, high={count}, medium={count}, low={count}, info={count}, false_positives={count}, new_regressions={count}, fixed_since_last={count}. Risk score: {score}/100.' -ForegroundColor Green
